title: Nemuaron vieillardii
taxonomy: Nemuaron vieillardii is classified in the family Nymphaeaceae and order Nymphaeales. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. This classification places it within the group of flowering plants, specifically those with magnolioid flowers. Further, Nemuaron vieillardii is known to be an aquatic plant, growing in or near water environments. Its classification suggests that it is a rare species native to New Caledonia, with limited information available on its characteristics.

intro: Nemuaron vieillardii, also known as Vieillard's woodrose or Vieillard's rosewood, is a plant species native to New Caledonia, characterized by its unique and ornamental flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its purported health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Nemuaron vieillardii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nemuaron vieillardii is often used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
